title: STANDARD FOR DRIED OR DEHYDRATED GARLIC CXS 347-2019

sections.section: STANDARD FOR DRIED OR DEHYDRATED GARLIC CXS 347-2019
sections.summary: Heading only: title of the standard for dried or dehydrated garlic (CXS 347-2019).
States the standard was adopted in 2019 and amended in 2022.

Describes the 2022 amendment which replaces the previous text on labelling of non-retail containers with a requirement that such labelling be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Applies this standard to dried or dehydrated garlic as spices or culinary herbs offered for direct consumption, as an ingredient in food processing, or for repacking, and excludes products intended for industrial processing.

Describes dried/dehydrated garlic as the dried cloves and/or bulb of Allium sativum L. and lists permissible styles (whole, cracked/broken, ground/powdered or other labelled styles) with particle size for pieces/powdered forms determined by contract.
Defines dried/dehydrated garlic as a finished product obtained by drying the cloves and/or bulb of garlic (Allium sativum L.).

Specifies allowable styles for dried garlic (whole, cracked/broken, ground/powdered or other labelled styles) and that particle size for pieces and ground/powdered forms is determined by contractual agreement between buyer and seller.

sections.section: 2.2.1 Dried roots, rhizomes and bulbs may be:
sections.summary: Allows dried roots, rhizomes and bulbs to be presented as whole (peeled intact garlic and/or cloves), cracked/broken, ground/powdered, or other distinct styles provided they are labelled accordingly.

sections.section: 2.2.2 The particle size of pieces and ground/powdered styles is determined by contractual agreement between buyer and seller
sections.summary: States that particle size of pieces and ground/powdered styles is to be determined by contractual agreement between buyer and seller.

Sets essential composition and quality factors: the product must conform to Section 2.1 and the chemical/physical requirements in Annexes I and II, blends may use the general name while a specific variety name requires at least 80% of that variety, the product must have characteristic aroma/colour/flavour free from foreign or musty taints, meet Annex specifications, contain no adulteration, and allowed defects must not affect appearance, keeping quality or presentation.

Requires the product to conform to the definition in Section 2.1 and to Annexes I and II; permits using a general name for blends but mandates at least 80% of a named variety when a specific name is used.

Specifies quality factors: the product must have characteristic aroma, colour and flavour without foreign or musty odours, and comply with chemical and physical requirements in Annexes I and II, with allowed defects not affecting overall quality, keeping or presentation.

Requires the product to have a characteristic aroma, colour and flavour (which may vary by geo-climatic factors or variety) and to be free from any foreign odour, colour or flavour, especially mustiness.

The product must meet the chemical requirements in Annex I and the physical requirements in Annex II; permitted defects must not impair general appearance, keeping quality or package presentation, and the product must not be adulterated.
Anticaking agents are permitted in the powdered form of the product only in accordance with Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995).
Products must comply with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Preparation and handling are recommended to follow relevant Codex hygiene texts (CXC 1-1969, CXC 75-2015, CXC 78-2017 and other codes of practice), and products should comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.
Containers shall be filled as practicable without impairing quality and must be consistent with an accurate declaration of contents.
Products must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), with the following specific provisions applying.
The product name must follow Section 2.1, may include a style indication per Section 2.2, and may list species, variety or cultivar on the label.

sections.section: 8.2.1 The name of the product shall be as described in Section 2.1.
sections.summary: The product name must be as described in Section 2.1.

sections.section: 8.2.2 The name of the product may include an indication of the style as described in Section 2.2.
sections.summary: The product name may include an indication of the style as described in Section 2.2.

sections.section: 8.2.3 Species, variety or cultivar may be listed on the label.
sections.summary: Species, variety or cultivar may be listed on the label.

sections.section: 8.3 Country of origin and country of harvest
sections.summary: Labels must indicate country of origin; country of harvest, region of harvest and year of harvest are optional declarations.

sections.section: 8.3.1 Country of origin shall be indicated.
sections.summary: Country of origin shall be indicated on the label.
Country of harvest may be indicated (optional).

sections.section: 8.3.3 Region of harvest and year of harvest (optional)
sections.summary: Region of harvest and year of harvest may be indicated optionally.
Commercial identification may include class/grade where applicable and may optionally indicate size.
Class/Grade should be indicated where applicable.
Size may be indicated (optional).
Labelling of non‑retail containers should comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers in Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Compliance testing must use the methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard, and a sampling plan is to be developed.
Methods and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions of this standard shall be used to check compliance.
Placeholder only — indicates the sampling plan is to be developed and contains no substantive requirements.

Annex specifying chemical-quality limits for dried/dehydrated garlic: moisture max 7.0% (powdered) or 8.0% (other), total ash (dry basis) max 6.0%, acid‑insoluble ash (dry basis) max 0.5%, volatile organic sulfur compounds (dry basis) min 0.3%, and cold‑water‑soluble extract (dry basis) 70–90%.
